*Section: THE BOOK OF TRANSFORMATION: God Momentum*

---

##    11\. God Momentum (Part IV): "40 Days And 40 Nights" Of Creativity 

If you decided to continue beyond the first 21 days...

Congratulations.

Between Day 22 - Day 40 is where you will seek to turn your new habits into a way of life.

**This is where you will begin your true transformation.**

_The first 3 weeks (21 days) was the rite of passage you had to make it through._

_You needed those 3 weeks to prove to yourself that "you can do it"._

_It's where you experience all the fundamental patterns and challenges of acquiring discipline._

_But it's also about as far as most people with "passion" get._

That's why I don’t trust people with “passion”.

I trust people who are “on a mission”.

You know how many people I see who start with “all guns blazing” ready to take over the world? It only lasts a few weeks before they’re back to their old habits again. I see those types of people coming from a mile away.

But the people who are on a mission? They're a lot quieter about things. They don’t trust themselves. Their human frailty worries them. That’s how important it is for them to succeed. They don’t want their own weaknesses to fuck them up.

That’s why you’ll see them surround themselves with the insurance policies and energy builders we discussed in the last chapter.

They do those things just to make sure they get things done.

They understand that their bouts with insomnia will come back again and again.

But weaker every time if they stay on course.

And their ego will come back again to make them believe they're strong enough to slack.

But that voice will get less loud if they stay aware and focused.

**Yes, the devil will come back over and again to tempt you. But you'll get stronger every time.**

You will go through this cycles over and over until this new momentum is a lifestyle.

# Until the person you're staring at in the mirror isn't the same person reading this today.

The journey is exhausting, but exhilarating.

The first 21 perfect days was an accomplishment... But from now on life will taste different.

You're writing your artist story in real-time now.

_A story that will become YOUR legend.  _

The 40 days bible story is the perfect allegory to describe every artist's journey on their road to greatness:

_*After being baptized by John the Baptist, Jesus goes out to the desert to fast. He is tempted by the devil for 40 days and 40 nights. Jesus refuses each temptation. One torment after another. Until the devil finally gives up. That's when Jesus the prophet is born. He then returns to Galilee to begin his ministry._

All worthwhile journeys follow this same pattern.

1\. Removal of yourself from society.

2\. Getting your sh-t together with the utmost discipline.

3\. Refusing all the destructive temptations you couldn't in the past. (Until you're swatting them like Neo's slow-motion bullets in the Matrix.)

4\. You become "the new you". Beautiful, powerful and unrecognizable to those who knew you before.

That...

Is God Momentum.

P.S. The beautiful thing about doing these "40 Days And 40 Nights of discipline" is:

Even if you go back to sucking afterwards… Whatever you did for that month and a half will keeps on paying dividends way into your future.

Not a bad ROI.

But please... Whatever you do... Don't fall into [this tragic God Momentum trap...](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/17758538)

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

And the wild thing about those 40 days is that the wilderness was all about identity. “If you really are…” that’s what He was up against. That’s what we are all up against. Every time you pick up a paintbrush you wonder, “am I an artist?” Every time you sit down to write— the barrage of “you don’t have this degree or that degree.” It’s honestly not about talent if you succeed- it’s about the tenacious will power to keep going, even while you still wonder if you have what it takes. the wilderness is always about finding freedom.

I've just finished my mini transformation ( 21 then 40 days back to back ) and all I can say is WOW - what a journey :D So much learned by showing up consistently day in, day out - the progress in my technique feels exponential and my once wobbly creative confidence feels way more grounded & solid now. I'm so grateful for everything I've absorbed so far & I'm itching to get back to my flow.

I’m on day 10 and those temptations are real. I see where I got stuck in complacency and why I haven’t moved forward in the past. I know I still have those challenges ahead of me, but for the first time I see them as a part of the journey. Not the reason to stop and start over again 10 years later (true story). I’m actually excited for what’s ahead, challenges and all. I finally see the forest AND the trees. And I am finally enjoying the journey. I’ll keep checking in.
